reasoning: The Lean statement exactly formalizes "V is not the union of finitely many proper subspaces over an infinite field." K is a Field with Infinite K (matches "infinite field F"), V is a K-module (which over a field means vector space), ι is a Fintype (matches "finitely many"), γ : ι → Submodule K V provides the family of subspaces, and γ i ≠ ⊤ captures "proper" (since submodules are always ≤ ⊤). The conclusion ⋃ i, (γ i : Set V) ≠ ⊤ correctly asserts the union is not all of V, because ⊤ : Set V unfolds to Set.univ (verified in Mathlib's OrderTop instance on Set) and ⊤ : Submodule K V also coerces to Set.univ (top_coe lemma). The universal quantification over all finite families of proper subspaces is the correct unfolding of the informal claim. No hypotheses have been silently added or dropped.
